prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_endpoint consumer_primary_1)
(claim_connector_for_endpoint consumer_primary_1 connector_1)
(bind_request_template_to_endpoint consumer_primary_1 api_request_template_1)
(finalize_endpoint_configuration consumer_primary_1 api_request_template_1)
(discover_and_signal_source_variant consumer_primary_1 source_variant_1 api_request_template_1)
(attach_credential_to_endpoint consumer_primary_1 credential_1)
(process_signaled_source_variant_primary consumer_primary_1 source_variant_1 credential_1)
(register_endpoint consumer_secondary_1)
(claim_connector_for_endpoint consumer_secondary_1 connector_2)
(release_request_template_from_endpoint consumer_primary_1 api_request_template_1)
(bind_request_template_to_endpoint consumer_secondary_1 api_request_template_1)
(finalize_endpoint_configuration consumer_secondary_1 api_request_template_1)
(discover_and_signal_destination_variant consumer_secondary_1 destination_variant_1 api_request_template_1)
(detach_credential_from_endpoint consumer_primary_1 credential_1)
(attach_credential_to_endpoint consumer_secondary_1 credential_1)
(process_signaled_destination_variant_secondary consumer_secondary_1 destination_variant_1 credential_1)
(release_request_template_from_endpoint consumer_secondary_1 api_request_template_1)
(bind_request_template_to_endpoint consumer_primary_1 api_request_template_1)
(create_execution_task consumer_primary_1 consumer_secondary_1 source_variant_1 destination_variant_1 execution_task_slot_1)
(mark_execution_task_scheduled execution_task_slot_1 consumer_primary_1 api_request_template_1)
(release_request_template_from_endpoint consumer_primary_1 api_request_template_1)
(register_endpoint component_instance_a)
(claim_connector_for_endpoint component_instance_a connector_3)
(bind_request_template_to_endpoint component_instance_a api_request_template_1)
(finalize_endpoint_configuration component_instance_a api_request_template_1)
(materialize_response_resource component_instance_a response_resource_1 execution_task_slot_1)
(confirm_response_resource_materialization component_instance_a response_resource_1 execution_task_slot_1 api_request_template_1)
(bind_worker_token_to_component component_instance_a worker_token_1)
(bind_timeout_profile_to_component component_instance_a timeout_profile_default)
(detach_credential_from_endpoint consumer_secondary_1 credential_1)
(attach_credential_to_endpoint component_instance_a credential_1)
(apply_worker_token_feature component_instance_a worker_token_1 credential_1 response_resource_1 execution_task_slot_1)
(enable_component_with_timeout_profile component_instance_a timeout_profile_default response_resource_1 execution_task_slot_1)
(finalize_component component_instance_a)
(attach_integration_context_to_endpoint component_instance_a integration_context_1 api_request_template_1)
(propagate_context_to_component_and_release_connector component_instance_a connector_3 integration_context_1)
(release_request_template_from_endpoint component_instance_a api_request_template_1)
(register_endpoint component_instance_b)
(claim_connector_for_endpoint component_instance_b connector_3)
(bind_request_template_to_endpoint component_instance_b api_request_template_1)
(finalize_endpoint_configuration component_instance_b api_request_template_1)
(materialize_response_resource component_instance_b response_resource_2 execution_task_slot_1)
(confirm_response_resource_materialization component_instance_b response_resource_2 execution_task_slot_1 api_request_template_1)
(release_worker_token_from_component component_instance_a worker_token_1)
(bind_worker_token_to_component component_instance_b worker_token_1)
(release_timeout_profile_from_component component_instance_a timeout_profile_default)
(bind_timeout_profile_to_component component_instance_b timeout_profile_default)
(detach_credential_from_endpoint component_instance_a credential_1)
(attach_credential_to_endpoint component_instance_b credential_1)
(apply_worker_token_feature component_instance_b worker_token_1 credential_1 response_resource_2 execution_task_slot_1)
(enable_component_with_timeout_profile component_instance_b timeout_profile_default response_resource_2 execution_task_slot_1)
(finalize_component component_instance_b)
(attach_integration_context_to_endpoint component_instance_b integration_context_1 api_request_template_1)
(propagate_context_to_component_and_release_connector component_instance_b connector_3 integration_context_1)
(release_request_template_from_endpoint component_instance_b api_request_template_1)
(propagate_result_to_primary_consumer consumer_primary_1 execution_task_slot_1)
(propagate_result_to_secondary_consumer consumer_secondary_1 execution_task_slot_1)
(bind_request_template_to_endpoint consumer_primary_1 api_request_template_1)
(attach_integration_context_to_endpoint consumer_primary_1 integration_context_1 api_request_template_1)
(propagate_context_to_consumer_and_release_connector consumer_primary_1 connector_1 integration_context_1)
(release_request_template_from_endpoint consumer_primary_1 api_request_template_1)
(bind_request_template_to_endpoint consumer_secondary_1 api_request_template_1)
(attach_integration_context_to_endpoint consumer_secondary_1 integration_context_1 api_request_template_1)
(propagate_context_to_secondary_and_release_connector consumer_secondary_1 connector_2 integration_context_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex with many objects and predicates. The goal is to achieve (integration_committed) for consumer_primary_1, consumer_secondary_1, component_instance_a, and component_instance_b.


## Examining Integration Goals
- The task involves achieving (integration_committed) status for multiple entities: consumer_primary_1, consumer_secondary_1, component_instance_a, and component_instance_b.
- The complexity arises from managing numerous objects and predicates to meet the integration goal.

## Examining Integration Goals
- Achieving (integration_committed) for all entities requires processing_result_committed and context_attached for each endpoint.
- Actions like attach_integration_context_to_endpoint need both processing_result_committed and bound_request_template.

## Planning Endpoint Setup
- Endpoints (consumer_primary_1, consumer_secondary_1, component_instance_a, component_instance_b) need registration and connector allocation to proceed.
- Only one api_request_template_1 is available, suggesting a sequential binding and releasing process for endpoints.

## Addressing Resource Constraints
- Shared resources like execution_task_slot_1 and credential pose challenges, requiring careful sequencing.
- Components and consumers need to be made ready, involving steps like registering endpoints and claiming connectors.

## Addressing Resource Constraints
- Only one api_request_template_1 and one credential are available, requiring sequential binding and releasing for endpoints.
- Credential attachment occurs after endpoints are ready, complicating the sequence for consumer_primary_1 and consumer_secondary_1.

## Sequencing Endpoint Configuration
- Start with registering and claiming a connector for consumer_primary_1, then bind and finalize with api_request_template_1 to make it ready.
- After signaling the source variant, attach credential_1 to enable process_signaled_source_variant_primary, leading to consumer_primary_ready.

## Exploring Readiness Paths
- Consumer_primary_ready can be achieved via process_signaled_source_variant_primary or finalize_primary_variant_handling, both requiring readiness and specific conditions.
- Data mapping, like data_mapping_1, is temporarily assigned during assign_data_mapping_to_consumer_primary but becomes available again after finalization.

## Planning Task Creation
- Creating an execution task requires both consumer_primary_1 and consumer_secondary_1 to be ready, with signaled source and destination variants.
- Execution_task_slot_1 is shared, suggesting a need for careful sequencing to avoid conflicts with other components.

## Configuring Endpoint Readiness
- Register and claim a connector for consumer_primary_1, then bind and finalize with api_request_template_1 to make it ready.
- Signal the source variant and attach credential_1 to process, achieving consumer_primary_ready with signal_set.

## Managing Shared Resources
- Release api_request_template_1 from consumer_primary_1 to make it available for consumer_secondary_1 after its initial use.
- Detach credential_1 from consumer_primary_1 to reattach it to consumer_secondary_1, ensuring both consumers can signal and become ready.

## Sequencing Task Creation
- After both consumers are ready with signaled variants, create an execution task using execution_task_slot_1, reserving it for the task.
- Release and rebind api_request_template_1 to consumer_primary_1 for scheduling, ensuring prerequisites like registration and connector allocation are met.

## Configuring Component Readiness
- Enable component_instance_a with timeout_profile_default and response_resource_1, ensuring modifier_applied and ready_for_finalization are set.
- Finalize component_instance_a directly since no feature_bindings are present, leading to processing_result_committed and completion_flag.

## Managing Component Dependencies
- Attach integration_context_1 to component_instance_a, requiring processing_result_committed and a bound request template, resulting in context_attached.
- Propagate context and release connector_3, making it available again and freeing integration_context_1 for further use.

## Sequencing Component Operations
- For component_instance_b, similar readiness steps are needed, but api_request_template_1 remains bound to component_instance_a, requiring release.
- Materialize response_2 for component_instance_b after ensuring it's ready, leveraging the shared execution_task_slot_1 for scheduling.

## Managing Template Availability  
- Release api_request_template_1 from component_instance_b to make it available for consumer use.  
- Ensure the template is unbound from component_instance_b after attachment, enabling reuse for consumers.  

## Binding Templates to Consumers  
- Bind api_request_template_1 to consumer_primary_1 after ensuring registration and connector allocation.  
- Attach integration_context_1 to consumer_primary_1, requiring the template to be bound and context available.  

## Finalizing Consumer Integration  
- Propagate context and release connector_1 for consumer_primary_1, marking it as committed and freeing resources.  
- Repeat the process for consumer_secondary_1, binding the template and propagating context with connector_2.  

## Verifying Action Specifications  
- Note that propagate_context_to_consumer_and_release_connector is specific to consumer_primary, with a separate action for consumer_secondary.  
- Confirm all four components and consumers are integration_committed, completing the sequence.

## Configuring Component Instance A
- Claim connector_3 for component_instance_a and bind api_request_template_1, preparing for configuration.
- Finalize endpoint configuration and materialize response_resource_1 using execution_task_slot_1, ensuring readiness.

## Managing Component Resources
- Bind worker_token_1 and timeout_profile_default to component_instance_a, applying worker token features with credential_1.
- Enable component_instance_a with timeout_profile_default and finalize, marking it processing_result_committed.

## Transferring Resources
- Detach credential_1 from consumer_secondary_1 and attach it to component_instance_a for use.
- Release worker_token_1 and timeout_profile_default from component_instance_a after finalization, preparing for reuse.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
